nodes.name: Force Equilibrium and Free Body Diagrams
nodes.name_en: Force Equilibrium and Free Body Diagrams
nodes.definition: Analysis of forces acting on a body in static equilibrium using free body diagrams to represent all external forces and moments.

nodes.name: Stress and Strain Fundamentals
nodes.definition: Fundamental concepts of normal stress, shear stress, normal strain, and shear strain, including Hooke's law and elastic behavior of materials.

nodes.name: Beam Bending and Shear Analysis
nodes.definition: Analysis of internal forces in beams including shear force diagrams, bending moment diagrams, and deflection calculations.

nodes.name: Column Buckling and Stability
nodes.definition: Critical load analysis for columns under axial compression, including Euler buckling formula and effective length concepts.

nodes.name: Truss and Frame Analysis
nodes.definition: Analysis of planar and space trusses using method of joints and method of sections, and rigid frame analysis.

nodes.name: Structural Dynamics and Vibration
nodes.definition: Dynamic response of structures under time-varying loads, including free vibration, forced vibration, and damping effects.

nodes.name: Seismic Design and Earthquake Engineering
nodes.definition: Design of structures to resist earthquake loads, including response spectrum analysis, ductile detailing, and seismic isolation techniques.

nodes.name: Wind Engineering and Aerodynamics
nodes.definition: Wind effects on structures including wind load calculation, vortex-induced vibration, and aeroelastic phenomena.

nodes.name: Reinforced Concrete Structure Design
nodes.definition: Design of reinforced concrete members including beams, columns, slabs, and walls according to limit state design principles.

nodes.name: Prestressed Concrete Design
nodes.definition: Design of prestressed concrete elements including pre-tensioning and post-tensioning systems, loss of prestress, and serviceability.

nodes.name: Steel Structure Design
nodes.definition: Design of steel structural members and connections including tension members, compression members, beams, and beam-columns.

nodes.name: High-Rise Building Structural Systems
nodes.definition: Structural systems for tall buildings including shear wall systems, frame-tube systems, outrigger systems, and composite structures.

nodes.name: Finite Element Method in Structures
nodes.definition: Application of finite element method to structural analysis including element formulation, assembly, and solution techniques.

nodes.name: Soil Classification and Properties
nodes.definition: Classification of soils based on particle size distribution, Atterberg limits, and engineering properties.

nodes.name: Soil Mechanics and Effective Stress
nodes.definition: Principles of effective stress, pore water pressure, and total stress relationships in saturated and unsaturated soils.

nodes.name: Soil Compaction and Permeability
nodes.definition: Soil compaction principles, Proctor test, permeability coefficients, Darcy's law, and seepage analysis.

nodes.name: Foundation Engineering and Design
nodes.definition: Design of shallow and deep foundations including bearing capacity analysis, settlement calculation, and pile foundation design.

nodes.name: Slope Stability Analysis
nodes.definition: Methods of slope stability analysis including limit equilibrium methods, circular and non-circular failure surfaces.

nodes.name: Earth Retaining Structures
nodes.definition: Design of retaining walls, sheet piles, and anchored structures based on lateral earth pressure theories.

nodes.name: Deep Excavation and Underground Structures
nodes.definition: Design and construction of deep excavations, bracing systems, diaphragm walls, and underground structures.

nodes.name: Ground Improvement Techniques
nodes.definition: Techniques for improving weak ground including grouting, soil reinforcement, geosynthetics, and preloading methods.

nodes.name: Rock Mechanics and Tunnel Engineering
nodes.definition: Properties and classification of rock masses, tunnel design methods, and rock support systems.

nodes.name: Engineering Material Properties
nodes.definition: Mechanical properties of construction materials including strength, stiffness, ductility, and standard testing methods.

nodes.name: Concrete Technology and Mix Design
nodes.definition: Composition, mix design, properties, and durability of concrete including use of admixtures and supplementary cementitious materials.

nodes.name: Structural Steel and Alloys
nodes.definition: Properties of structural steels, alloy designations, weldability, fatigue behavior, and corrosion protection.

nodes.name: Timber and Masonry Structures
nodes.definition: Properties and design of timber and masonry structural systems including connections and load paths.

nodes.name: High-Performance and Smart Materials
nodes.definition: Advanced construction materials including high-performance concrete, fiber-reinforced polymers, self-healing materials, and smart materials.

nodes.name: Construction Methods and Equipment
nodes.definition: Major construction methods, equipment selection, formwork systems, and scaffolding for building and infrastructure projects.

nodes.name: Engineering Surveying and Measurement
nodes.definition: Surveying methods for construction including leveling, triangulation, GPS positioning, and GIS applications.

nodes.name: Construction Project Planning and Scheduling
nodes.definition: Project planning techniques including critical path method, PERT, resource leveling, and schedule optimization.

nodes.name: Construction Quality and Safety Management
nodes.definition: Quality assurance, quality control, safety regulations, and risk management in construction projects.

nodes.name: Construction Project Management
nodes.definition: Comprehensive project management including cost control, contract administration, BIM coordination, and lean construction principles.

nodes.name: Road Geometric Design
nodes.definition: Geometric design of roads including horizontal and vertical alignment, sight distance, and cross-section design.

nodes.name: Pavement Design and Materials
nodes.definition: Design of flexible and rigid pavements including material selection, structural analysis, and performance prediction.

nodes.name: Traffic Engineering and Planning
nodes.definition: Traffic flow theory, capacity analysis, signal design, intersection layout, and transportation planning.

nodes.name: Bridge Types and Structural Analysis
nodes.definition: Classification and structural analysis of bridge types including beam, arch, truss, cable-stayed, and suspension bridges.

nodes.name: Bridge Design Codes and Load Combinations
nodes.name_en: Bridge Design Codes and Load Combinations
nodes.definition: Bridge design according to national codes including load combinations, vehicle load models, and fatigue considerations.

nodes.name: Long-Span Bridge Engineering
nodes.definition: Design and construction of long-span bridges including cable systems, aerodynamic stability, and structural health monitoring.

nodes.name: Pavement Maintenance and Rehabilitation
nodes.definition: Pavement condition assessment, maintenance strategies, rehabilitation techniques, and life-cycle cost analysis.

nodes.name: Intelligent Transportation Systems
nodes.definition: Intelligent transportation systems including connected vehicles, smart infrastructure, traffic management, and autonomous driving.

nodes.name: Architectural Drawing and Representation
nodes.definition: Architectural drawing conventions including plans, elevations, sections, and modern digital representation tools.

nodes.name: Building Design Principles
nodes.definition: Fundamental principles of architectural design including space planning, circulation, form-function relationships, and design methodology.

nodes.name: Building Codes and Regulations
nodes.definition: Building codes and regulations including fire safety, accessibility, structural requirements, and zoning laws.

nodes.name: Building Services and MEP Systems
nodes.definition: Mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems in buildings including HVAC, water supply, drainage, and electrical distribution.

nodes.name: Sustainable and Green Building Design
nodes.definition: Sustainable building design principles including energy efficiency, green building certifications, passive design strategies, and life-cycle assessment.

nodes.name: Fluid Mechanics for Civil Engineers
nodes.definition: Fundamental fluid mechanics for civil engineering including hydrostatics, Bernoulli equation, pipe flow, and open channel flow.

nodes.name: Water Supply and Distribution Systems
nodes.definition: Design of water supply systems including intake, treatment, storage, distribution networks, and pump stations.

nodes.name: Stormwater and Drainage Engineering
nodes.definition: Design of stormwater management and drainage systems including rainfall analysis, runoff estimation, and sewer design.

nodes.name: Water and Wastewater Treatment
nodes.definition: Water and wastewater treatment processes including coagulation, filtration, disinfection, biological treatment, and sludge management.
